intel_overview.headline_zh: 欧盟AI版权立法提速，UPC调解机制落地
intel_overview.headline_en: EU AI Copyright Reform and UPC ADR Launch
intel_overview.summary_zh: 本季度欧洲IP态势呈现“立法攻坚”与“司法优化”双轨并行。欧盟委员会确认针对生成式AI的版权立法干预，填补政策盲点；统一专利法院（UPC）正式启用PMAC调解系统及案件管理平台，强化替代性争议解决能力。同时，德国恶意商标申请激增与英国FRAND费率裁决凸显执法风险。EPO推进无纸化及质量提升，利摩日瓷器获首个手工业地理标志，显示制度创新与保护范围扩展同步加速。
top_signals: 欧盟确认启动生成式AI版权专项立法，独立于现有指令审查
UPC PMAC调解程序规则生效且案件管理系统正式上线
德国DPMA警示AI辅助恶意商标注册风险显著上升
英国高等法院在华为案中确立SEP临时许可及中点费率法
cross_currents: 技术治理与权利平衡：AI既被用于加速恶意商标抢注，又成为版权立法亟需规制的核心对象，监管与技术博弈加剧
争议解决多元化：UPC引入ADR机制与英国法院深耕FRAND费率裁判，形成诉讼与调解互补的立体维权生态
保护范畴扩张：从传统专利商标向手工业地理标志及数据合规延伸，IP资产边界与欧盟价值观绑定更紧密
implications: 涉欧AI企业需紧急评估训练数据版权合规性，应对即将出台的欧盟专项立法限制
在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应优先评估利用UPC新设PMAC调解机制以降低维权成本与时间
出口企业需警惕德国等地利用AI工具进行的恶意商标抢注，建立早期监测与异议机制
watchlist: 欧盟生成式AI版权立法草案的具体文本及退出机制设计
UPC上诉法院判例被纳入EPO审查指南后的具体引用标准
英国高等法院在SEP案件中“中点法”费率计算的后续判例效应

daily_report.headline_zh: 欧盟拟立法干预AI版权，UPC程序规则明确化
daily_report.headline_en: EU Targets AI Copyright; UPC Clarifies Procedures
daily_report.executive_line: 欧盟确认将针对生成式AI进行独立版权立法干预；UPC上诉庭确立英语听证先例并厘清初步异议边界；德国恶意商标申请风险显著上升。
daily_report.summary_zh: 本窗口期核心动态集中在立法与司法程序两端。欧盟委员会计划绕过现有指令审查，直接立法解决生成式AI对版权框架的冲击。司法层面，统一专利法院（UPC）通过两项关键裁定：允许在对方同意下使用英语进行口头听证，以及严格限定初步异议仅适用于管辖权等程序事项，旨在提升审判效率。此外，德国专利商标局警告恶意商标注册激增，EUIPO则拒绝“George Orwell”商标注册，强化名人姓名描述性认定标准。

storylines.title_en: EUIPO Refuses George Orwell Trademark Registration
storylines.what_happened_en: The EUIPO Grand Board refused registration of GEORGE ORWELL for books and films, ruling the name describes content rather than indicating commercial origin.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: This clarifies that famous names face refusal risks for media goods if perceived as descriptive subject matter, requiring strong evidence of origin function.

storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 增强诉讼灵活性并防止程序拖延，确立效率优先原则，为后续案件审理提供明确程序指引。

report_items.title_en: UKIPO Guidance on Identifying Counterfeit Fashion in Second-Hand Markets
report_items.title: Guidance: How to spot fake fashion when shopping second-hand
report_items.summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布指南，旨在帮助消费者理解如何识别在转售平台上销售的假冒产品，并提供举报相关 listings 的具体步骤。该文件属于官方发布的操作指引，聚焦于二手市场中的知识产权侵权识别与应对流程。
report_items.summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published guidance to assist consumers in identifying counterfeit fashion items sold through second-hand resale platforms. The document outlines practical steps for recognizing fake products and provides instructions on how to report suspicious listings. This resource aims to enhance awareness of trademark infringement risks within the secondary market, offering a structured approach for rights holders and buyers to address potential counterfeiting issues on digital marketplaces.
core_points_zh: 原文摘要显示，指南核心目的是帮助识别转售平台上的假冒产品
原文明确指出，指南包含举报可疑 listings 的具体操作步骤
core_points_en: Guidance helps identify counterfeit fashion products on resale platforms.
Document outlines specific steps for reporting suspicious listings.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则指引给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This guidance highlights regulatory attention to trademark enforcement in the growing second-hand economy. For brand owners, it underscores the importance of clear consumer education and accessible reporting mechanisms. Monitoring how platforms integrate these identification tips into their compliance frameworks may reveal evolving standards for intermediary liability and user-generated content moderation in the UK.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands should review if their anti-counterfeiting materials align with UKIPO identification criteria for secondary markets.
Practice note: The guidance emphasizes consumer participation in enforcement by providing clear reporting pathways for suspicious listings.
What to monitor: Observe whether resale platforms update their terms or detection tools in response to this official guidance.

report_items.title: A Blind Spot at the Heart of EU Copyright and AI Policymaking?
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟委员会发布证据征集令，确认计划对欧盟版权框架进行新的立法干预，重点解决生成式AI带来的挑战。该举措独立于正在进行的《数字单一市场版权指令》审查，后者预计不会重新开放文本。新倡议涵盖AI挑战、在线盗版、录音制品及研究例外等四个领域，旨在改善欧洲创意与创新环境。
report_items.summary_en: The European Commission has published a call for evidence confirming plans for new legislative intervention in the EU copyright framework. This targeted initiative, distinct from the ongoing review of the Copyright in the Digital Single Market Directive, aims to address challenges linked to artificial intelligence and other specific areas. The Commission expects measures to tackle market and technological developments associated with generative AI. While the Directive review proceeds in parallel without reopening its text, this new initiative covers four domains: AI-related creator challenges...
core_points_zh: 欧盟委员会确认计划进行新的立法干预，以应对生成式AI对版权框架造成的破坏
此次针对AI的措施独立于《数字单一市场版权指令》的平行审查，后者预计不修改原文
现有文本挖掘例外未能平衡技术发展与控制权，退出机制未成为有效的许可杠杆
core_points_en: Commission confirms new legislative intervention targeting AI challenges, separate from the ongoing CDSM Directive review process.
Initiative covers four areas: AI impacts, live event piracy, third-country sound recordings, and research exception harmonization.
Existing text and data mining exceptions failed to balance technology development with rightholder control and value participation.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类政策更新给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This development indicates that EU policymakers prefer targeted legislative fixes over comprehensive directive revisions to address generative AI. Rights holders and technology developers should anticipate new rules governing training data usage and opt-out mechanisms. The separation from the CDSM review suggests a faster, albeit potentially fragmented, regulatory approach to emerging AI copyright issues.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and AI developers must prepare for new compliance obligations regarding training data and creator compensation models outside the current CDSM fra…
Practice note: The initiative’s broad scope beyond AI suggests potential legislative bundling, which may complicate negotiations and extend timelines for final adoption.
What to monitor: Watch for specific proposals on clarifying the InfoSoc research exception and introducing an EU-level secondary publication right alongside AI measures.

report_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册风险日益加剧
report_items.title_en: Bad Faith in German Trademark Filings: DPMA Reports Rising Risk
report_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (Bad Faith) In Trademark Filings: A Growing Risk For Businesses
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法下的“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念。DPMA指出自2025年中以来疑似恶意申请显著增加，常利用AI工具锁定未注册商标以施加竞争压力。恶意注册违反《德国商标法》第8条及第50条，可在审查或无效程序中予以驳回或宣告无效。
report_items.summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) reports a significant increase in bad faith trademark applications since mid-2025. These filings often target signs used by third parties without formal protection, aiming to exert competitive pressure rather than indicate commercial origin. Contributing factors include AI tools that identify unregistered signs and online marketplace enforcement mechanisms that allow swift blocking of competitors. Under the German Trademark Act, bad faith is grounds for refusing registration or declaring invalidity. Rights holders are advised to secure registered...
core_points_zh: DPMA称2025年中起恶意申请激增，旨在利用未注册商标施压竞争对手
AI工具与电商平台执法机制被滥用，加速了对第三方的封锁与干扰
依据德国商标法，恶意申请可被驳回，已注册商标亦可被宣告无效
core_points_en: DPMA notes a rise in bad faith filings targeting unregistered signs used by third parties since mid-2025.
AI tools and marketplace enforcement mechanisms facilitate strategic registrations aimed at obstructing competitors.
German law excludes bad faith applications from registration and allows post-registration invalidation under specific statutory provisions.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Bad faith objections are increasingly central in German practice, aligning with EU-level developments. The DPMA’s focus on abusive filings highlights the need for proactive portfolio management. Companies should prioritize early registration of key brands and implement monitoring systems to detect and challenge speculative applications before they leverage platform enforcement tools against legitimate market particip...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early registration and active monitoring are critical to prevent third parties from leveraging bad faith filings for competitive advantage.
Practice note: Bad faith challenges rely on proving abusive intent, such as obstructing third parties, under Section 8(2) No. 14 and Section 50(1) of the German Trademark Act.
What to monitor: Watch for evolving DPMA examination practices and Federal Court of Justice case law regarding bad faith in the context of AI-driven identification and digital enf…

report_items.title_zh: 智能球门、护腿板及捡球机器人：DPMA更新足球技术画廊
report_items.title_en: DPMA Expands Football and Technology Gallery for 2026 World Cup
report_items.title: Smarte Tore, intelligente Schienbeinschoner und Roboter, die Bälle sammeln
report_items.summary_zh: 德国专利商标局（DPMA）借2026年美加墨世界杯之机，扩展其“足球与技术”在线画廊。该画廊收录了全球范围内过去125年间与足球相关的专利文献，涵盖门线技术、视频助理裁判、生物数据监测传感器及AI驱动设备等创新。DPMA旨在通过展示这些发明，强调知识产权保护在挖掘足球领域经济潜力中的重要作用。
report_items.summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) has expanded its online gallery, Football and Technology, to coincide with the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico, and the USA. The updated collection features patent documents spanning 125 years of innovation, ranging from early mechanical equipment like screw-in studs to modern digital solutions. New additions include AI-controlled safety nets, ball-collecting robots, smart goals, and intelligent shin guards. The gallery also details established technologies such as goal-line technology and video assistant refereeing. DPMA President Eva Schew...
core_points_zh: DPMA为配合2026年世界杯，更新了展示全球足球相关专利的在线画廊
展出内容涵盖从传统机械装备到AI、机器人及生物数据监测等现代技术创新
DPMA主席指出，该画廊证实专利保护是利用足球发明经济潜力的重要工具
core_points_en: DPMA updates its online gallery with recent patents including AI-driven safety nets and robotics.
The collection covers 125 years of football innovations, from mechanical gear to digital tech.
The initiative highlights the role of IP protection in maximizing the economic value of sports inventions.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This update serves primarily as a public awareness initiative rather than a legal precedent. For rights holders, it underscores the DPMA’s view of IP as a key driver for commercializing sports technology. Practitioners may find value in reviewing how the office categorizes emerging fields like wearable sensors and AI-assisted officiating within historical patent contexts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Demonstrates the DPMA's strategic emphasis on IP as a tool for commercializing sports-related innovations and protecting R&D investments.
Practice note: The gallery offers a structured view of how mechanical and digital football technologies are documented, aiding prior art searches in sports equipment sectors.
What to monitor: Observe if future updates include detailed case studies on the patentability boundaries of AI-driven officiating or real-time biometric data processing.

report_items.title_zh: 如何在欧洲专利局提起异议
report_items.title_en: Procedural Guide to Filing Oppositions at the European Patent Office
report_items.title: How To File An Opposition At The European Patent Office (EPO)
report_items.summary_zh: 本文介绍向欧洲专利局提起专利异议的程序。异议须书面或电子提交并缴费，仅限依据《欧洲专利公约》第100条规定的理由，包括缺乏可专利性、公开不充分及新增主题。明确排除清晰度、单一性等非独立异议理由。
report_items.summary_en: This guidance outlines the procedural requirements for filing oppositions against granted European patents at the EPO. Opponents must submit a notice in writing or electronically via Online Filing 2.0, ensuring the official fee is paid simultaneously. Challenges are strictly limited to grounds under Article 100 EPC: lack of patentability, insufficient disclosure, or added subject matter. The text clarifies that exclusions such as mathematical methods or medical treatments fall under patentability checks. It emphasizes that each ground must be clearly identified during filing, reinforcing that...
core_points_zh: 异议须向欧洲专利局书面或电子提交，并在提交时缴纳官方费用
异议理由限于公约第100条：缺乏可专利性、公开不充分或新增主题
清晰度、单一性或优先权无效等问题不得作为独立异议理由提出
core_points_en: Opposition notices must be filed in writing or electronically with the official fee paid at submission.
Valid challenges are restricted to Article 100 EPC grounds: patentability, disclosure sufficiency, or added matter.
Patentability grounds include lack of novelty, inventive step, or excluded subject matter like medical methods.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则指引给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This overview reinforces the strict statutory limits of EPO opposition proceedings. Practitioners must ensure arguments align exclusively with Article 100 EPC grounds, avoiding inadmissible issues like clarity. Proper fee payment and clear ground identification are critical procedural steps to maintain admissibility and effectively challenge patent validity in Europe.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Understanding the narrow scope of allowable opposition grounds helps rights holders assess the viability of challenging competitor patents.
Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid upon filing and that arguments strictly adhere to Article 100 EPC to avoid inadmissibility.
What to monitor: Track EPO updates to Online Filing 2.0 and any evolving case law interpreting Article 100 grounds for sufficient disclosure.

report_items.title_en: EUIPO Grand Board Refuses GEORGE ORWELL Trademark for Content-Related Goods
report_items.title: The Price Of Literary Fame – EUIPO Grand Board Refuses Registration Of “GEORGE ORWELL” As A Trademark
report_items.summary_zh: 2025年12月19日，EUIPO大上诉委员会裁定，“GEORGE ORWELL”文字商标不得在书籍、电影及相关娱乐教育服务上注册为欧盟商标。委员会认定，相关公众会将该名称视为描述商品内容或主题，而非商业来源指示，因此依据《欧盟商标条例》第7(1)(c)条以缺乏显著性/具有描述性为由驳回申请。
report_items.summary_en: The EUIPO Grand Board of Appeal refused registration of the word mark GEORGE ORWELL for books, films, and educational services. In decision R 2248/2019-G, the Board ruled that the relevant public perceives the name as describing the subject matter or content of the goods, rather than indicating commercial origin. Consequently, the mark was rejected under Article 7(1)(c) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation for lack of distinctiveness due to descriptiveness. This ruling clarifies that famous persons’ names may be deemed descriptive when applied to content-oriented products, establishing that such si...
core_points_zh: EUIPO大上诉委员会拒绝“GEORGE ORWELL”在书籍及娱乐服务上的欧盟商标注册
裁定认定名人姓名可被公众视为描述商品内容或主题，而非商业来源指示
确认“内容/主题”属于欧盟商标条例第7(1)(c)条下描述性的认可子类别
core_points_en: Grand Board confirms subject matter is a recognized subcategory of descriptiveness under Article 7(1)(c).
Famous names describing content rather than origin are unregistrable for books and media.
Decision resolves diverging Office practices regarding registrability of well-known persons’ names.
report_items.insight_zh: 本案澄清了名人姓名在内容导向型商品上的可注册性标准。若姓名被公众感知为描述内容而非来源，将因描述性被驳回。企业在申请含名人姓名的商标时，需评估其在特定类别中是否仅起内容指示作用，避免触犯绝对驳回理由。
report_items.insight_en: This decision establishes that famous names used on content-driven goods may be refused as descriptive. Rights holders should anticipate higher scrutiny for personal names in publishing and entertainment classes. Applicants must demonstrate that the sign functions primarily as a brand identifier, not merely as a topic indicator, to overcome absolute grounds for refusal in these sectors.
insight_points_zh: 名人姓名在书籍、影音等内容类产品上易被认定为描述性标志
需区分姓名作为来源标识与作为内容主题指示的不同法律后果
后续应关注EUIPO对其他类似名人姓名商标申请的审查尺度一致性
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Names of famous figures face heightened refusal risks for books, films, and educational services where the name signals content topic.
Practice note: Evidence must prove the mark indicates commercial origin, countering the presumption that the name describes the work’s subject matter.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO examinations of other historical or literary figures’ names for similar content-oriented goods and services.

report_items.title_en: CRISPR-Cas9 in Europe: Current Patent Landscape and Future Outlook
report_items.title: CRISPR‑Cas9 in Europe: the Current Situation and What Comes Next
report_items.summary_zh: 原文仅提供标题及摘要片段，指出CRISPR/Cas9技术引发复杂专利战。2012至2013年间多方申请导致权利重叠，形成两大主要竞争阵营。因正文缺失，无法提供具体法律状态或后续走向的详细事实。
report_items.summary_en: The emergence of CRISPR-Cas9 technology has triggered one of the most complex patent disputes in recent history. Initial filings by multiple groups between 2012 and 2013 resulted in overlapping claims, leading to prolonged legal conflicts over rights. Two primary rival camps have dominated this landscape. This analysis reviews the current status of these European patent battles and outlines potential future developments for stakeholders monitoring this high-stakes biotechnology sector.
core_points_zh: CRISPR/Cas9技术引发近年最复杂的专利纠纷之一
年多方申请导致权利要求重叠及长期法律争端
争端中形成了两个主要的对立竞争阵营
core_points_en: CRISPR-Cas9 discovery sparked complex patent battles due to overlapping filings from 2012 to 2013.
Two principal rival camps emerged as key competitors in the struggle for patent rights.
Years of legal wrangling have defined the current European landscape for this biotechnology platform.
report_items.insight_zh: 鉴于原文仅为导语片段，缺乏具体案件进展、法院裁决或欧洲专利局审查动态等实质内容。目前仅能确认该技术在欧洲存在长期的权属争议背景，需等待全文发布以获取关于“当前状况”及“未来走向”的具体法律分析或实务建议。
report_items.insight_en: Given the limited source text, specific legal outcomes or procedural updates are not detailed. However, the historical context of overlapping claims remains critical for rights holders. Stakeholders should recognize that the European patent environment for CRISPR-Cas9 is still shaped by these foundational disputes. Monitoring the resolution of conflicts between the two main rival camps is essential for understanding...
insight_points_zh: 原文未提供具体的专利有效性或侵权判定结论
需关注后续全文是否披露欧洲专利局或法院的最新动态
目前仅能确认存在两大竞争阵营的历史背景事实
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must account for the legacy of overlapping 2012-2013 filings when assessing portfolio strength in Europe.
Practice note: The existence of two dominant rival camps suggests continued complexity in interpreting claim scope and validity.
What to monitor: Future publications should be tracked for specific updates on European Patent Office decisions or court rulings affecting these rival groups.

report_items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭准许口头听证使用英语的裁定
report_items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Permits English Oral Pleadings Despite French Language of Proceedings
report_items.title: CoA, May 15, 2026, Order On Request For Use Of English At Oral Hearing, UPC-CoA-50/2026
report_items.summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Robert Bosch诉Valeo案中作出裁定，尽管法语为案件程序语言，但鉴于上诉人代表法语口语能力不足且被上诉人同意，法院基于程序效率与公平原则，准许双方在2026年5月26日的口头听证中使用英语进行辩论。法院引用先例及程序规则前言作为法律依据，强调灵活性与成本效益，未正式变更案件语言。
report_items.summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal authorized oral pleadings in English for a hearing where French remained the formal language of proceedings. The decision addressed an appellant’s representative who understood French but lacked sufficient oral proficiency for effective advocacy. The court prioritized procedural efficiency and fairness, noting that direct English advocacy was superior to using an interpreter. Crucially, the respondent consented to the request, which strengthened the application. The court relied on the Rules of Procedure Preamble’s principles of flexibility and cost-effectiveness, citin...
core_points_zh: 法院基于程序规则前言的灵活性原则，准许在非程序语言的口头听证中使用英语
被上诉人的同意显著增强了变更听证语言的请求效力，使附属请求变得不必要
法院参考了涉及相同当事人的先前程序裁定，将其作为支持当前决定的说服性先例
core_points_en: The Court permitted English oral pleadings while retaining French as the formal language of proceedings based on pragmatic efficiency considerations.
Respondent consent to the language request significantly strengthened the application, rendering subsidiary requests for partial English use unnecessary.
The Court cited the Rules of Procedure Preamble and prior orders involving the same parties as the legal basis for this flexible approach.
report_items.insight_zh: 该裁定确立了UPC上诉庭在特定条件下允许庭审语言与程序语言分离的实务操作。当事人可通过证明语言障碍、获得对方同意并援引效率原则，申请使用更熟练的语言进行口头辩论。这为跨国诉讼中的语言策略提供了灵活性，同时强调了双方合意对程序决定的关键影响。
report_items.insight_en: This order highlights the UPC’s willingness to decouple the language of oral hearings from the formal language of proceedings to ensure effective representation. It demonstrates that party consensus on procedural logistics can drive judicial discretion, particularly when supported by principles of efficiency. Practitioners should note that prior procedural history between identical parties may influence subsequent ca...
insight_points_zh: 程序语言与庭审口语可分离，需证明语言能力不足及对方同意
程序规则前言中的效率与公平原则是法院行使裁量权的主要依据
相同当事人间的先前程序裁定可作为后续程序决定的重要参考因素
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties facing language proficiency gaps can seek oral hearing adjustments without formally changing the case language, provided opposing counsel consents.
Practice note: Citing the Rules of Procedure Preamble on efficiency and referencing prior procedural orders between the same parties strengthens requests for flexible hearing arra…
What to monitor: Whether the Court of Appeal continues to treat party alignment on procedural matters as a decisive factor in granting language accommodations.

report_items.title_zh: 统一专利法院初步异议：管辖权与实体问题的明确界限
report_items.title_en: UPC Preliminary Objections: Strict Boundary Between Jurisdiction and Substance
report_items.title: Preliminary Objections Before The UPC: A Clear Boundary Between Jurisdiction And Substance
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析统一专利法院在REEL International诉Fives ECL案中的裁定。法院依据《程序规则》第19.1条，驳回被告基于诉讼利益和德国判决既判力提出的初步异议。法院明确初步异议仅限管辖权、分庭管辖权及语言三类情形，其他实体争议应合并审理以符合程序经济原则。
report_items.summary_en: In a dispute between REEL International and Fives ECL, the Unified Patent Court rejected a defendant’s preliminary objection based on lack of interest to sue and res judicata from German proceedings. Relying on Rule 19.1 of the Rules of Procedure, the Court clarified that preliminary objections are strictly limited to challenges regarding court jurisdiction, division competence, or language of proceedings. The Court held that issues such as standing and res judicata require substantive examination of facts and evidence, rather than serving as procedural filters. This decision reinforces a clea...
core_points_zh: UPC程序规则限定初步异议仅适用于管辖权、分庭管辖权及诉讼语言三类情形
诉讼利益及既判力抗辩不属初步异议范围，需结合事实证据进行实体审查
法院拒绝分离审理或中间命令请求，主张单次听证以提升效率并实现一年内结案
core_points_en: Rule 19.1 limits preliminary objections exclusively to jurisdiction, division competence, and language issues.
Claims regarding interest to sue and res judicata fall outside the scope of preliminary objections.
Substantive defenses require full factual examination and cannot be resolved via procedural dismissal.
report_items.insight_zh: UPC通过严格界定初步异议范围，防止程序碎片化，强调诉讼经济与快速裁决。当事人需注意管辖权异议与实体抗辩的程序分流，避免试图通过初步异议拖延实体审理。此裁判思路有助于确立UPC高效审理的司法惯例。
report_items.insight_en: This ruling confirms the UPC’s commitment to procedural efficiency by preventing the fragmentation of cases through broad preliminary objections. Practitioners should note that defenses like res judicata will be addressed during merits proceedings, not as threshold jurisdictional bars. This approach reduces delays but requires parties to prepare comprehensive factual arguments early in the litigation process.
insight_points_zh: 明确初步异议的封闭列表性质，排除诉讼利益等实体前置抗辩
确立合并审理原则，拒绝将既判力等问题从实体审理中剥离
观察UPC后续案件是否持续贯彻这一效率优先的程序管理逻辑
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Expect faster initial jurisdictional rulings, but anticipate that substantive defenses like res judicata will proceed to full trial rather than causing early dis…
Practice note: Do not rely on Rule 19.1 for non-jurisdictional defenses; prepare detailed factual evidence for merits hearings instead of seeking procedural shortcuts.
What to monitor: Future UPC case law to confirm if this strict interpretation of Rule 19.1 remains consistent across different divisions and fact patterns.

report_items.title_en: Planning Successful IP Mediation: EUIPO, UPC, and WIPO Mechanisms
report_items.title: How To Plan Successful IP Mediation

report_items.summary_en: Mediation offers faster, confidential, and commercially focused resolutions for intellectual property disputes compared to litigation. The World Intellectual Property Organization Arbitration and Mediation Center reported a forty-two percent case increase in 2025, with small and medium-sized enterprises comprising fifty-nine percent of parties. In the European Union, the EUIPO Mediation Centre expanded its services in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre is scheduled to ina...
core_points_zh: EUIPO调解中心服务于2025年6月扩展，覆盖所有欧盟商标和注册设计的双方程序
统一专利法院专利调解与仲裁中心定于2026年6月成立，提供欧洲替代性争议解决论坛
WIPO数据显示2025年案件量增42%，其中中小企业和个人当事人占比达59%
core_points_en: WIPO AMC cases rose forty-two percent in 2025, with SMEs and individuals accounting for fifty-nine percent of involved parties.
EUIPO Mediation Centre services expanded in June 2025 to include all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.
The Unified Patent Court Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre will launch in June 2026 as a new European ADR forum.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则指引给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: The expansion of institutional mediation frameworks at EUIPO and the upcoming UPC center signals a strategic shift toward non-adversarial dispute resolution in Europe. Rights holders should evaluate these mechanisms for cross-border conflicts where confidentiality and preserving commercial relationships are prioritized over public judicial precedents. The high participation of SMEs in WIPO cases suggests mediation is...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Mediation provides confidential, commercially oriented solutions for multi-jurisdictional IP disputes, particularly valuable for SMEs and entities seeking to pre…
Practice note: Counsel should assess the expanded EUIPO mediation scope for trademark and design oppositions, and prepare for the UPC Center's June 2026 launch for patent-related…
What to monitor: Track procedural rules and adoption rates of the new UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre following its inauguration in June 2026.

daily_report.executive_line_en: The EU initiates standalone AI copyright legislation while the UPC streamlines proceedings through English hearing precedents and strict preliminary objection boundaries, alongside rising bad faith trademark risks in Ger...
daily_report.summary_en: This reporting period highlights significant legislative and procedural developments across European intellectual property. The European Commission confirmed plans for independent legislative intervention to address generative AI challenges within the copyright framework, moving beyond existing directive reviews. In judicial proceedings, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal established key precedents by permitting English oral pleadings with party consent and strictly limiting preliminary objections to jurisdictional matters, thereby enhancing procedural efficiency. Trademark enforcement sees heightened scrutiny, as the EUIPO Grand Board refused registration of the George Orwell mark for content-related goods, reinforcing descriptiveness standards for famous names. Concurrently, the German Patent and Trade Mark Office reported a surge in bad faith applications targeting unregistered signs, driven by automated tools. These shifts necessitate updated compliance strategies for AI training data, proactive monitoring of trademark portfolios in Germany, and strategic use of flexible language options in UPC litigation to optimize defense and prosecution outcomes across the region.

report_items.title_zh: 指南：如何在二手购物时识别假冒时尚商品
report_items.title_en: UKIPO Guidance on Identifying Counterfeit Fashion in Second-Hand Markets
report_items.title: Guidance: How to spot fake fashion when shopping second-hand
report_items.summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布指南，旨在帮助消费者理解如何识别在转售平台上销售的假冒产品，并提供举报相关 listings 的具体步骤。该文件属于官方发布的操作指引，聚焦于二手市场中的知识产权侵权识别与应对流程。
report_items.summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published guidance to assist consumers in identifying counterfeit fashion items sold through second-hand resale platforms. The document outlines practical steps for recognizing fake products and provides instructions on how to report suspicious listings. This resource aims to enhance awareness of trademark infringement risks within the secondary market, offering a structured approach for rights holders and buyers to address potential counterfeiting issues on digital marketplaces.
core_points_zh: 原文摘要显示，指南核心目的是帮助识别转售平台上的假冒产品
原文明确指出，指南包含举报可疑 listings 的具体操作步骤
core_points_en: Guidance helps identify counterfeit fashion products on resale platforms.
Document outlines specific steps for reporting suspicious listings.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则指引给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This guidance highlights regulatory attention to trademark enforcement in the growing second-hand economy. For brand owners, it underscores the importance of clear consumer education and accessible reporting mechanisms. Monitoring how platforms integrate these identification tips into their compliance frameworks may reveal evolving standards for intermediary liability and user-generated content moderation in the UK.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands should review if their anti-counterfeiting materials align with UKIPO identification criteria for secondary markets.
Practice note: The guidance emphasizes consumer participation in enforcement by providing clear reporting pathways for suspicious listings.
What to monitor: Observe whether resale platforms update their terms or detection tools in response to this official guidance.

report_items.title_zh: 欧盟版权与AI政策制定中的盲点？
report_items.title_en: European Commission Launches Targeted Copyright Initiative Addressing Generative AI Challenges
report_items.title: A Blind Spot at the Heart of EU Copyright and AI Policymaking?
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟委员会发布证据征集令，确认计划对欧盟版权框架进行新的立法干预，重点解决生成式AI带来的挑战。该举措独立于正在进行的《数字单一市场版权指令》审查，后者预计不会重新开放文本。新倡议涵盖AI挑战、在线盗版、录音制品及研究例外等四个领域，旨在改善欧洲创意与创新环境。
report_items.summary_en: The European Commission has published a call for evidence confirming plans for new legislative intervention in the EU copyright framework. This targeted initiative, distinct from the ongoing review of the Copyright in the Digital Single Market Directive, aims to address challenges linked to artificial intelligence and other specific areas. The Commission expects measures to tackle market and technological developments associated with generative AI. While the Directive review proceeds in parallel without reopening its text, this new initiative covers four domains: AI-related creator challenges...
core_points_zh: 欧盟委员会确认计划进行新的立法干预，以应对生成式AI对版权框架造成的破坏
此次针对AI的措施独立于《数字单一市场版权指令》的平行审查，后者预计不修改原文
现有文本挖掘例外未能平衡技术发展与控制权，退出机制未成为有效的许可杠杆
core_points_en: Commission confirms new legislative intervention targeting AI challenges, separate from the ongoing CDSM Directive review process.
Initiative covers four areas: AI impacts, live event piracy, third-country sound recordings, and research exception harmonization.
Existing text and data mining exceptions failed to balance technology development with rightholder control and value participation.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类政策更新给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This development indicates that EU policymakers prefer targeted legislative fixes over comprehensive directive revisions to address generative AI. Rights holders and technology developers should anticipate new rules governing training data usage and opt-out mechanisms. The separation from the CDSM review suggests a faster, albeit potentially fragmented, regulatory approach to emerging AI copyright issues.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and AI developers must prepare for new compliance obligations regarding training data and creator compensation models outside the current CDSM fra…
Practice note: The initiative’s broad scope beyond AI suggests potential legislative bundling, which may complicate negotiations and extend timelines for final adoption.
What to monitor: Watch for specific proposals on clarifying the InfoSoc research exception and introducing an EU-level secondary publication right alongside AI measures.

report_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册风险日益加剧
report_items.title_en: Bad Faith in German Trademark Filings: DPMA Reports Rising Risk
report_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (Bad Faith) In Trademark Filings: A Growing Risk For Businesses
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法下的“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念。DPMA指出自2025年中以来疑似恶意申请显著增加，常利用AI工具锁定未注册商标以施加竞争压力。恶意注册违反《德国商标法》第8条及第50条，可在审查或无效程序中予以驳回或宣告无效。
report_items.summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) reports a significant increase in bad faith trademark applications since mid-2025. These filings often target signs used by third parties without formal protection, aiming to exert competitive pressure rather than indicate commercial origin. Contributing factors include AI tools that identify unregistered signs and online marketplace enforcement mechanisms that allow swift blocking of competitors. Under the German Trademark Act, bad faith is grounds for refusing registration or declaring invalidity. Rights holders are advised to secure registered...
core_points_zh: DPMA称2025年中起恶意申请激增，旨在利用未注册商标施压竞争对手
AI工具与电商平台执法机制被滥用，加速了对第三方的封锁与干扰
依据德国商标法，恶意申请可被驳回，已注册商标亦可被宣告无效
core_points_en: DPMA notes a rise in bad faith filings targeting unregistered signs used by third parties since mid-2025.
AI tools and marketplace enforcement mechanisms facilitate strategic registrations aimed at obstructing competitors.
German law excludes bad faith applications from registration and allows post-registration invalidation under specific statutory provisions.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Bad faith objections are increasingly central in German practice, aligning with EU-level developments. The DPMA’s focus on abusive filings highlights the need for proactive portfolio management. Companies should prioritize early registration of key brands and implement monitoring systems to detect and challenge speculative applications before they leverage platform enforcement tools against legitimate market particip...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early registration and active monitoring are critical to prevent third parties from leveraging bad faith filings for competitive advantage.
Practice note: Bad faith challenges rely on proving abusive intent, such as obstructing third parties, under Section 8(2) No. 14 and Section 50(1) of the German Trademark Act.
What to monitor: Watch for evolving DPMA examination practices and Federal Court of Justice case law regarding bad faith in the context of AI-driven identification and digital enf…

report_items.title_zh: 欧专局讨论2026指南用户咨询结果及2027修订方向
report_items.title_en: EPO SACEPO Reviews 2026 Guidelines Consultation and Outlines 2027 Updates
report_items.title: SACEPO WP/G discusses outcomes of user consultation on the Guidelines 2026
report_items.summary_zh: 欧专局SACEPO工作组于5月12日会议讨论2026版审查指南（含EPC、PCT-EPO及统一专利指南）的用户咨询反馈。咨询共获307条意见，主要关注EPC指南。会议还展望2027版更新，包括支持无纸化流程的法律变更、可能强制要求指明修改依据，以及引入统一专利法院上诉法院判例。此外，讨论了G 1/24决定实施、抗体创造性、分案申请及异议程序等议题。
report_items.summary_en: The SACEPO Working Party on Guidelines discussed feedback from the public consultation on the 2026 EPC, PCT-EPO, and Unitary Patent Guidelines. The consultation received 307 comments, primarily from European patent representatives focusing on the EPC Guidelines. The EPO outlined planned updates for the 2027 edition, supporting the transition to a fully paperless granting process by April 2027. Proposed changes include potential obligations to indicate the basis for amendments during European phase entry and opposition proceedings. Additionally, the EPO intends to incorporate references to Unif...
core_points_zh: 指南咨询获307条反馈，主要涉及EPC指南，反映用户积极参与
版指南拟支持2027年4月全面无纸化，或强制要求指明修改依据
计划在EPC指南中引用统一专利法院上诉法院判例，并持续讨论G 1/25实施
core_points_en: SACEPO reviewed 307 user comments on the 2026 Guidelines, with significant focus on EPC examination practices.
The 2027 Guidelines will support full paperless processing and may mandate indicating amendment bases in specific proceedings.
EPO plans to reference UPC Court of Appeal decisions in the EPC Guidelines and clarify G 1/24 implementation.
report_items.insight_zh: 欧专局正通过指南修订强化程序简化与质量管控，特别是适应无纸化转型及统一专利法院判例体系。从业者需密切关注2027版指南关于修改依据指明的潜在义务，以及UPC判例对EPC审查实践的具体影响，同时留意抗体可专利性及分案异议程序的后续澄清。
report_items.insight_en: Practitioners should prepare for procedural shifts in the 2027 Guidelines, particularly regarding mandatory amendment bases and UPC case law integration. The inclusion of UPC decisions signals closer alignment between EPO practice and unitary patent jurisprudence. Monitoring the final text on amendment requirements is essential for drafting strategies in European phase entries and oppositions.
insight_points_zh: 关注2027指南中关于进入欧洲阶段及异议程序中指明修改依据的潜在强制义务
监测UPC上诉法院判例被纳入EPC指南后的具体引用方式及对审查标准的影响
留意抗体创造性、分案申请及异议辅助请求准入等议题在收敛实践计划下的后续更新
insight_points_en: Client relevance: The integration of UPC Court of Appeal decisions into EPO Guidelines may influence claim interpretation and validity assessments across both systems.
Practice note: Anticipate stricter formal requirements for indicating amendment bases during European phase entry and opposition proceedings under the 2027 revisions.
What to monitor: Final wording of the 2027 Guidelines regarding G 1/25 implementation and the specific scope of UPC case law references.

report_items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局SACEPO质量工作组最新会议亮点
report_items.title_en: EPO SACEPO Quality Meeting Highlights: 2026 Plan and AI Integration
report_items.title: Highlights from the latest meeting of the SACEPO Working Party on Quality
report_items.summary_zh: 欧洲专利局于2026年5月8日举办第16届SACEPO质量工作组会议，71名代表参会。会议重点讨论2026年质量行动计划进展、人工智能在提升审查质量中的应用及风险管控。局长强调质量为核心优先事项，并介绍了AI辅助起草工具等内部开发项目，旨在增强效率同时确保人类决策责任。
report_items.summary_en: The European Patent Office hosted the 16th SACEPO Working Party on Quality meeting on 8 May 2026, with 71 global representatives. Discussions centered on the 2026 Quality Action Plan, reviewing 2025 achievements in prior art access and examination harmonization. The EPO detailed its integration of artificial intelligence tools to enhance quality and efficiency, including applications in pre-search, oral proceedings minutes, and stakeholder queries. A new AI-supported drafting assistant for examiners was also highlighted. Participants acknowledged progress in consolidating best practices within...
core_points_zh: 会议回顾2025年质量成果，包括现有技术获取改善及审查实践协调进展
推进2026年质量行动计划，巩固主动检索部门最佳实践并加强异议部门学习
整合AI工具支持预检索及文书起草，坚持以人为本，确保审查员承担最终决策责任
core_points_en: EPO reviewed 2025 quality results, highlighting improvements in prior art access and examination practice harmonization.
The 2026 Quality Action Plan focuses on consolidating Active Search Division best practices and opposition training.
AI tools are being integrated for pre-search, drafting assistance, and legal platform responses to boost efficiency.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: The EPO is actively embedding AI into examination workflows to support, not replace, human decision-making. For practitioners, this signals potential shifts in the speed and structure of office actions. While efficiency gains are anticipated, maintaining rigorous argumentation remains critical as examiners utilize new drafting aids. Stakeholders should observe how these tools influence the consistency of examination...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: AI-assisted examination may accelerate procedural steps, requiring timely and precise responses to maintain prosecution momentum.
Practice note: Examiners using AI drafting assistants may produce more standardized office actions; clear, distinct arguments remain essential for overcoming rejections.
What to monitor: Track the impact of AI tools on examination consistency and the EPO’s future guidance on algorithmic transparency in decision-making.

report_items.title_zh: EPO成功完成ISO监督审核
report_items.title_en: EPO Maintains ISO Certifications Following Successful Surveillance Audit
report_items.title: EPO successfully completes ISO surveillance audit
report_items.summary_zh: 2026年4月，欧洲专利局（EPO）顺利通过外部审计机构的管理体系ISO监督审核。此次审核确认了EPO在质量管理、职业健康安全、信息安全和环境管理方面的稳健性，维持了其ISO 9001、ISO 45001、ISO 27001及ISO 14001认证。这体现了EPO对国际标准及合规性的承诺，并通过综合管理体系支持高质量服务交付。
report_items.summary_en: In April 2026, the European Patent Office (EPO) successfully completed its periodic ISO surveillance audit conducted by external auditors. The review confirmed the continued robustness and maturity of the EPO’s integrated management systems, allowing the organization to maintain its certifications for quality management (ISO 9001), occupational health and safety (ISO 45001), information security (ISO 27001), and environmental management (ISO 14001). This outcome underscores the EPO’s adherence to international standards and regulatory compliance. By aligning policies, processes, and controls w...
core_points_zh: EPO于2026年4月通过外部审计机构的ISO管理体系监督审核
维持ISO 9001、45001、27001及14001四项国际认证，确认体系成熟稳健
通过综合管理体系整合政策流程，确保持续改进及服务交付质量
core_points_en: The EPO passed its April 2026 external ISO surveillance audit, confirming ongoing adherence to international management standards.
Certifications maintained include ISO 9001 for quality, ISO 27001 for information security, ISO 45001 for safety, and ISO 14001 for environment.
The EPO utilizes an Integrated Management System to unify policies and controls, supporting service quality and continuous operational improvement.
report_items.insight_zh: 此新闻属于EPO内部行政与合规动态，不直接改变专利实体法或审查标准。实务中需关注其信息安全（ISO 27001）与质量管理（ISO 9001）的持续合规，这间接保障了电子提交系统及审查流程的稳定性与数据安全性，是评估EPO服务可靠性的基础指标。
report_items.insight_en: This update confirms the administrative and operational stability of the EPO rather than introducing changes to substantive patent law or examination guidelines. For rights holders and legal practitioners, the maintenance of ISO 27001 and ISO 9001 certifications serves as a key indicator of the reliability and data security standards underpinning the EPO’s electronic filing systems and procedural workflows. While thi...
insight_points_zh: Client relevance/客户相关性: EPO运营稳定性与数据安全合规性保障
Practice note/实务观察: 关注EPO管理体系认证状态对服务连续性的影响
What to monitor/后续观察: 无具体法律变动，仅需知悉机构合规状态更新
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Confirms the stability and data security compliance of EPO operations, indirectly supporting the reliability of electronic submission channels and case processin…
Practice note: Monitor the status of EPO management system certifications as baseline indicators for service continuity and operational maturity.
What to monitor: No immediate legal or procedural changes; treat as routine compliance confirmation regarding institutional operational standards.

report_items.title_zh: 智能球门、护腿板及捡球机器人：DPMA更新足球技术画廊
report_items.title_en: DPMA Expands Football and Technology Gallery for 2026 World Cup
report_items.title: Smarte Tore, intelligente Schienbeinschoner und Roboter, die Bälle sammeln
report_items.summary_zh: 德国专利商标局（DPMA）借2026年美加墨世界杯之机，扩展其“足球与技术”在线画廊。该画廊收录了全球范围内过去125年间与足球相关的专利文献，涵盖门线技术、视频助理裁判、生物数据监测传感器及AI驱动设备等创新。DPMA旨在通过展示这些发明，强调知识产权保护在挖掘足球领域经济潜力中的重要作用。
report_items.summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) has expanded its online gallery, Football and Technology, to coincide with the 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico, and the USA. The updated collection features patent documents spanning 125 years of innovation, ranging from early mechanical equipment like screw-in studs to modern digital solutions. New additions include AI-controlled safety nets, ball-collecting robots, smart goals, and intelligent shin guards. The gallery also details established technologies such as goal-line technology and video assistant refereeing. DPMA President Eva Schew...
core_points_zh: DPMA为配合2026年世界杯，更新了展示全球足球相关专利的在线画廊
展出内容涵盖从传统机械装备到AI、机器人及生物数据监测等现代技术创新
DPMA主席指出，该画廊证实专利保护是利用足球发明经济潜力的重要工具
core_points_en: DPMA updates its online gallery with recent patents including AI-driven safety nets and robotics.
The collection covers 125 years of football innovations, from mechanical gear to digital tech.
The initiative highlights the role of IP protection in maximizing the economic value of sports inventions.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: This update serves primarily as a public awareness initiative rather than a legal precedent. For rights holders, it underscores the DPMA’s view of IP as a key driver for commercializing sports technology. report_items.summary_en: This guidance outlines the procedural requirements for filing oppositions against granted European patents at the EPO. Opponents must submit a notice in writing or electronically via Online Filing 2.0, ensuring the official fee is paid simultaneously. Challenges are strictly limited to grounds under Article 100 EPC: lack of patentability, insufficient disclosure, or added subject matter. The text clarifies that exclusions such as mathematical methods or medical treatments fall under patentability checks. It emphasizes that each ground must be clearly identified during filing, reinforcing that...

report_items.title: The Price Of Literary Fame – EUIPO Grand Board Refuses Registration Of “GEORGE ORWELL” As A Trademark
report_items.summary_zh: 2025年12月19日，EUIPO大上诉委员会裁定，“GEORGE ORWELL”文字商标不得在书籍、电影及相关娱乐教育服务上注册为欧盟商标。委员会认定，相关公众会将该名称视为描述商品内容或主题，而非商业来源指示，因此依据《欧盟商标条例》第7(1)(c)条以缺乏显著性/具有描述性为由驳回申请。
report_items.summary_en: The EUIPO Grand Board of Appeal refused registration of the word mark GEORGE ORWELL for books, films, and educational services. In decision R 2248/2019-G, the Board ruled that the relevant public perceives the name as describing the subject matter or content of the goods, rather than indicating commercial origin. Consequently, the mark was rejected under Article 7(1)(c) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation for lack of distinctiveness due to descriptiveness. This ruling clarifies that famous persons’ names may be deemed descriptive when applied to content-oriented products, establishing that such si...
core_points_zh: EUIPO大上诉委员会拒绝“GEORGE ORWELL”在书籍及娱乐服务上的欧盟商标注册
裁定认定名人姓名可被公众视为描述商品内容或主题，而非商业来源指示
确认“内容/主题”属于欧盟商标条例第7(1)(c)条下描述性的认可子类别
core_points_en: Grand Board confirms subject matter is a recognized subcategory of descriptiveness under Article 7(1)(c).
Famous names describing content rather than origin are unregistrable for books and media.
Decision resolves diverging Office practices regarding registrability of well-known persons’ names.
report_items.insight_zh: 本案澄清了名人姓名在内容导向型商品上的可注册性标准。若姓名被公众感知为描述内容而非来源，将因描述性被驳回。企业在申请含名人姓名的商标时，需评估其在特定类别中是否仅起内容指示作用，避免触犯绝对驳回理由。
report_items.insight_en: This decision establishes that famous names used on content-driven goods may be refused as descriptive. Rights holders should anticipate higher scrutiny for personal names in publishing and entertainment classes. Applicants must demonstrate that the sign functions primarily as a brand identifier, not merely as a topic indicator, to overcome absolute grounds for refusal in these sectors.
insight_points_zh: 名人姓名在书籍、影音等内容类产品上易被认定为描述性标志
需区分姓名作为来源标识与作为内容主题指示的不同法律后果
后续应关注EUIPO对其他类似名人姓名商标申请的审查尺度一致性
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Names of famous figures face heightened refusal risks for books, films, and educational services where the name signals content topic.
Practice note: Evidence must prove the mark indicates commercial origin, countering the presumption that the name describes the work’s subject matter.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO examinations of other historical or literary figures’ names for similar content-oriented goods and services.

daily_audio_latest.headline_zh: 欧盟拟立法干预AI版权，UPC程序规则明确化
daily_audio_latest.headline_en: EU Targets AI Copyright; UPC Clarifies Procedures
daily_audio_latest.summary_zh: 本窗口期核心动态集中在立法与司法程序两端。欧盟委员会计划绕过现有指令审查，直接立法解决生成式AI对版权框架的冲击。司法层面，统一专利法院（UPC）通过两项关键裁定：允许在对方同意下使用英语进行口头听证，以及严格限定初步异议仅适用于管辖权等程序事项，旨在提升审判效率。此外，德国专利商标局警告恶意商标注册激增，EUIPO则拒绝“George Orwell”商标注册，强化名人姓名描述性认定标准。
daily_audio_latest.summary_en: This reporting period highlights significant legislative and procedural developments across European intellectual property. The European Commission confirmed plans for independent legislative intervention to address generative AI challenges within the copyright framework, moving beyond existing directive reviews. In judicial proceedings, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal established key precedents by permitting English oral pleadings with party consent and strictly limiting preliminary objections to jurisdictional matters, thereby enhancing procedural efficiency. Trademark enforcement sees heightened scrutiny, as the EUIPO Grand Board refused registration of the George Orwell mark for content-related goods, reinforcing descriptiveness standards for famous names. Concurrently, the German Patent and Trade Mark Office reported a surge in bad faith applications targeting unregistered signs, driven by automated tools. These shifts necessitate updated compliance strategies for AI training data, proactive monitoring of trademark portfolios in Germany, and strategic use of flexible language options in UPC litigation to optimize defense and prosecution outcomes across the region.

key_points: 中国创新主体地位提升：2025年中国首次进入EPO申请来源国前三，统一专利制度在中小企业中采纳率显著增长。
UPC司法标准明确化：最新判例确立严格证据前置披露规则，审查档案陈述将限制权利要求解释，初步异议范围被严格限定。
争议解决机制多元化：PMAC程序规则生效且案件管理系统上线，为SEP/FRAND纠纷提供调解仲裁新途径，降低维权成本。
actions: 复盘历史审查档案：确保授权过程中的意见陈述与当前诉讼主张一致，避免因自认导致保护范围被狭义解释。
优化证据管理流程：建立严格的证据前置披露机制，避免迟交证据被排除；评估利用PMAC调解机制解决跨境纠纷的可行性。
调整欧洲布局策略：结合中国申请量增长趋势，针对核心市场评估统一专利性价比，避免在单一高风险分院过度集中诉讼。
watchlist: UPC上诉法院关于管辖权范围提请欧盟法院裁决的最终结果
2027年EPO指南修订中关于修改依据强制指明的具体落地要求
杜塞尔多夫等新设合议庭对当地专利侵权判定倾向的潜在影响
topics.summary_en: In the first half of 2026, China ranked among the top three sources of European Patent Office applications, while Unitary Patent adoption reached 28.7 percent. The Unified Patent Court released its annual report and operationalized the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre’s case management system. However, recent judicial practice indicates stringent requirements for evidence disclosure, claim construction, and procedural compliance. To manage increasing caseloads, key divisions such as Düsseldorf have expanded judicial capacity. Rights holders must mitigate risks associated with centralized enforcement by ensuring litigation strategies align with prosecution histories. The maturation of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms offers new pathways for resolving cross-border and standard-essential patent disputes, potentially reducing litigation costs for multinational portfolios.
key_points_en: China entered the top three EPO filing countries in 2025, coinciding with significant Unitary Patent uptake among small and medium-sized enterprises.
UPC jurisprudence now enforces strict pre-trial evidence disclosure and limits claim interpretation based on prosecution history estoppel.
The PMAC procedural rules and digital case management system are live, providing structured mediation and arbitration options for SEP and FRAND disputes.
actions_en: Audit prosecution files to ensure consistency between past examiner responses and current litigation positions, preventing narrow claim construction.
Implement rigorous early evidence disclosure protocols to avoid exclusion of late submissions and evaluate PMAC mediation for cross-border conflicts.
Recalibrate European portfolio strategies by assessing Unitary Patent cost-benefit ratios and diversifying litigation venues to avoid concentration in high-risk divisions.
watchlist_en: Final outcome of the UPC Court of Appeal referral to the CJEU regarding jurisdictional scope.
Specific implementation requirements for mandatory amendment bases in the 2027 EPO Guidelines revisions.
Potential shifts in patent infringement adjudication trends following the expansion of panels at the Düsseldorf Local Division.

topics.headline_zh: 欧SEP司法碎片化与费率冲突加剧
topics.headline_en: European SEP Litigation Fragmentation Intensifies Amid Jurisdictional Conflicts
topics.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，欧洲SEP诉讼呈现英德UPC三足鼎立态势。英国法院确立全球费率裁判权并支持仲裁转向，德国及UPC则强化禁令救济与反诉禁令。中英法院在三星/中兴案中作出冲突的全球费率判决，凸显管辖权博弈。Avanci等专利池加速向汽车及Wi-Fi领域扩张，中国车企面临更高合规压力。
key_points: 司法管辖博弈：英国高院裁定全球FRAND费率并推动仲裁，UPC签发反反诉禁令限制中国法院临时许可，司法碎片化导致中英判决直接冲突。
行业许可扩张：Avanci专利池覆盖延伸至Wi-Fi 6/7及商用车领域，七家中国车企已签署4G/5G许可，诺基亚、华为等在流媒体与汽车领域积极维权。
程序规则收紧：UPC及德国法院明确FRAND抗辩中实施者需即时表达许可意愿，否则面临禁令风险；英国上诉法院确认含仲裁条款要约可中止诉讼。
actions: 审查许可协议中UPC管辖退出机制及仲裁条款，评估英国法院全球费率裁决对现有合同的冲击。
建立SEP前置谈判策略，收到侵权通知须立即书面表达许可意愿并提供担保，以保留FRAND抗辩权。
针对汽车及IoT产品，提前评估Avanci等专利池许可成本，权衡单独谈判与加入专利池的合规风险。
watchlist: 英国最高法院关于专利池FRAND义务及Unwired Planet后续案件的最终裁决风向。
UPC上诉法院在Dyson v Dreame案中关于长臂管辖界限的CJEU转介结果。
欧盟委员会SEP新规落地后，对标准必要性检查及许可透明度要求的执行细则。

topics.china_impact_summary_zh: 这条长窗显示，SEP 风险已经从传统通信终端延伸到车载与 IoT，并在德国、英国和 UPC 三条诉讼线同步抬升。对中国企业来说，下一阶段重点不只是单案应对，而是把许可准备、诉讼预案和规则跟踪并行推进。
china_action_points: 涉及车载、蜂窝、Wi-Fi 或 IoT 出海业务的中国企业，应尽快核查专利池与双边许可覆盖，避免产品上市后再被动补谈。
在德国、英国和 UPC 有销售、供应链或客户落点的企业，应同步准备 FRAND 谈判材料、可比许可证据与禁令应对预案。
法务、标准与商务团队需要持续跟踪英欧 SEP 政策和竞争法变化，避免沿用单一市场的许可策略应对欧洲。
针对已进入欧洲市场的中国终端、部件与整车企业，建议把 SEP 许可预算、争议升级路径和管辖博弈纳入年度经营安排。

topics.summary_en: During the first half of 2026, European standard-essential patent litigation exhibited significant fragmentation across the UK, Germany, and the Unified Patent Court. UK courts asserted authority to set global FRAND rates and encouraged arbitration, while German courts and the UPC strengthened injunctive relief and issued anti-anti-suit injunctions. Conflicting global rate judgments in cases involving major technology firms highlight intensifying jurisdictional competition. Concurrently, patent pools like Avanci expanded into Wi-Fi and automotive sectors, increasing compliance pressures on manufacturers. Procedural standards tightened, with the UPC requiring immediate expressions of licensing willingness to preserve FRAND defenses. These developments necessitate strategic adjustments in license negotiations, jurisdiction selection, and risk management for companies operating in connected...
key_points_en: Jurisdictional conflicts intensified as UK courts set global FRAND rates while the UPC issued anti-anti-suit injunctions against foreign interim licenses, creating direct legal con...
Licensing demands expanded as patent pools extended coverage to Wi-Fi 6/7 and commercial vehicles, prompting increased settlement activity among automotive manufacturers.
Procedural rules tightened, with UPC and German courts requiring implementers to immediately express licensing willingness upon notice to avoid injunction risks and preserve FRAND...
actions_en: Review license agreements for UPC opt-out mechanisms and arbitration clauses to assess exposure to conflicting global rate determinations from UK courts.
Establish proactive SEP negotiation protocols that ensure immediate written expression of licensing willingness and provision of security to maintain FRAND defense eligibility.
Evaluate licensing costs for automotive and IoT products by comparing patent pool participation against individual negotiation risks and compliance obligations.
watchlist_en: Final rulings from the UK Supreme Court regarding patent pool FRAND obligations and subsequent Unwired Planet legacy cases.
Outcomes of CJEU referrals by the UPC Court of Appeal concerning jurisdictional limits in cross-border SEP disputes.
Implementation details of new EU SEP regulations focusing on standard essentiality checks and licensing transparency requirements.

topics.headline_zh: EUIPO强化执法与融资，警惕恶意注册
topics.headline_en: EUIPO Strengthens Enforcement and IP Finance Amid Rising Bad Faith Risks
topics.summary_zh: 本季度EUIPO聚焦打击假冒与IP融资，联合欧盟委员会及EIF推出新举措。司法层面明确脱欧后英国权利失效，并严审名人商标显著性。德国恶意注册风险激增，需警惕AI滥用。手工艺GI制度落地，调解机制扩展至设计领域，企业应利用SME基金降低成本并优化合规策略。
key_points: 执法与融资：EUIPO联合EC打击网假，携手EIF解锁IP融资；IPEP数字工具强化实时执法协作。
司法确权风向：法院裁定脱欧后纯英国未注册商标不得异议欧盟商标；名人姓名在内容类商品易被拒注。
风险与机遇：德国恶意申请激增；手工艺GI制度实施（利摩日瓷器首例）；SME基金可补贴申请成本。
actions: 排查英国权利：复核依赖英国未注册商标的欧盟布局，避免因脱欧过渡期结束导致异议失败。
利用资助与调解：申请EUIPO SME基金降低注册成本；遇纠纷优先使用扩展后的调解中心解决。
防御恶意抢注：监测德国及欧盟市场，针对AI辅助的恶意囤积行为提前准备“恶意”抗辩证据。
watchlist: 德国DPMA对恶意注册认定的最新配套细则与执法口径变化。
欧盟手工艺和工业地理标志（CIGI）后续注册案例及保护范围界定。
EUIPO与EIF合作的IP支持型融资具体落地产品及对中企的适用性。
topics.summary_en: The EUIPO has intensified efforts against counterfeiting through joint initiatives with the European Commission and Europol, while partnering with the European Investment Fund to facilitate IP-backed financing. Judicial developments clarify that UK unregistered rights cannot support EU trademark oppositions post-Brexit transition, and strict descriptiveness standards apply to celebrity names in content-related goods. Concurrently, the German Patent and Trade Mark Office reports a surge in bad faith filings, often aided by AI tools targeting unprotected signs. The new framework for craft and industrial geographical indications is now operational, with the first advisory board convened. Rights holders should leverage expanded SME support funds and mediation services to optimize compliance costs and resolve disputes efficiently.
key_points_en: EUIPO collaborates with EU institutions to combat online counterfeiting and unlock IP-backed finance via the European Investment Fund.
Courts confirm UK unregistered rights lack standing in post-transition EU oppositions and reject celebrity marks describing content rather than origin.
Bad faith trademark filings are rising in Germany, while new craft geographical indication protections and SME funding opportunities become available.
actions_en: Audit EU trademark strategies relying on UK unregistered rights, as these no longer constitute valid grounds for opposition after the Brexit transition period.
Utilize EUIPO SME funds for cost reduction and prioritize the expanded mediation center for efficient dispute resolution in trademark and design matters.
Monitor German and EU markets for AI-driven bad faith registrations and prepare evidence of abusive intent to support invalidation actions.
watchlist_en: Evolution of DPMA guidelines and enforcement practices regarding bad faith trademark registrations and AI-assisted filing strategies.
Precedents set under the new EU craft and industrial geographical indications framework, particularly concerning scope of protection and registrability criteria.
Specific financial products and eligibility requirements emerging from the EUIPO and EIF partnership for IP-backed lending and investment.

topics.headline_zh: 欧英强化假冒执法与在线打击
topics.headline_en: EU and UK Strengthen Counterfeit Enforcement and Online IP Protection
topics.summary_zh: 2026年春，欧盟与英国显著升级知识产权执法力度。EUIPO联合欧盟委员会聚焦在线假冒打击，并支持SHIELD VI等跨国行动；英国UKIPO则延长IP犯罪部门资金至2029年并计划扩编。中欧执法协作紧密化，针对药品、耗材等重点领域的监管趋严，企业需警惕合规风险。
key_points: 欧盟层面：EUIPO与欧盟委员会联手打击网络假冒，并通过EMPACT框架强化跨机构协作及公众沟通。
英国动态：UKIPO确认IP犯罪专项执法资金延至2029年，拟扩编团队以应对日益增长的假冒风险。
重点领域：执法行动重点覆盖假冒药品（SHIELD VI行动）及办公耗材，强调溯源与有组织犯罪关联。
actions: 建立全链条合规溯源体系，确保出口产品（特别是药品、电子耗材）来源清晰，防范供应链指控。
加强英国及欧盟市场的渠道尽职调查，避免通过不明来源低价采购或分销，切断潜在侵权链接。
密切关注EUIPO与欧盟委员会后续发布的在线侵权配套指引，及时调整电商平台运营合规策略。
watchlist: 欧盟层面针对在线假冒打击的后续具体配套安排及明确执法口径。
英国UKIPO关于IP犯罪部门扩编的具体时间表、职能范围及2029年前执法数据变化。
EMPACT框架下知识产权犯罪执法的优先事项变化及跨机构协作新动态。
topics.topic_name_zh: 假冒执法 / 品牌保护
topics.summary_en: During spring 2026, the European Union and United Kingdom significantly intensified intellectual property enforcement efforts. The EUIPO and European Commission launched a joint initiative targeting online counterfeiting, while reinforcing cross-agency collaboration through the EMPACT framework. Support for Operation SHIELD VI highlighted a focused crackdown on counterfeit medicines and illicit doping substances. Concurrently, the UK Intellectual Property Office secured funding for its specialist IP Crime Unit until 2029, with plans to expand operational capacity amid rising counterfeit risks. Authorities also increased scrutiny on counterfeit office consumables, emphasizing links to organized crime. Rights holders must navigate stricter regulatory environments in both jurisdictions.
